ops.text: RESOLVED (ccx crash-loop papercut, 2026-07-14): root cause was NOT a ccx bug — concurrent Claude sessions' SessionStart hooks raced `brew upgrade yasyf/tap/ccx` on the shared keg, and any process exec'ing /opt/homebrew/bin/ccx mid-replacement died SIGKILL (CODESIGNING "Code Signature Invalid"); 26 crashes 05:24–08:13. Fixed in cc-context v0.17.3 (commit 626e9b18698b + release 2b772d2d61f4): install-binary fragment serializes the brew upgrade/install arms under an atomic mkdir lock at $DATA_DIR/brew.lock — owner-token-gated release, mv-aside stale break at 600s, reap-on-sight aside scavenging, version re-check under the lock, bounded ~10s arm-4 wait. Guarantee is deliberately bounded: N concurrent fresh sessions fully serialize (the crash-storm case); after a crashed holder, stale-break/release are best-effort and degrade to the pre-lock behavior bounded by Homebrew's own locks (codex-confirmed the residual generation races are unclosable in POSIX sh; accepted). Verified: two concurrent invocations with a stubbed 2s brew → exactly one upgrade, both exit 0; stale-lock break + foreign-token survival probes pass.
